premise: I poured my entire $140,000 inheritance from my mother into renovating my father’s run‑down lakeside lodge in exchange for his written promise of 50% ownership; when he remarried, my new stepmother forged a deed gifting the $520,000 property to her son from a previous marriage and evicted me on Christmas Eve. I uncovered the fraudulent deed, sued for quiet title, and when they dissolved their marriage during litigation, I bought my stepmother’s interest for $1 and took sole ownership — I now run the lodge as a women’s writing retreat, with a bonfire pit where I burn copies of their wedding photos every solstice.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Stepmother Stole My Lodge Via Forged Deed, Evicted Me On Christmas. So I Got
